title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  27 - 2018: Roman Colosseum Stairs

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder early in the morning, describing his progress building a 3D model of the Roman Colosseum in Google Earth, including reconstructing the staircase layout. He also discusses a self-made biweekly history study tool where he randomly selects decades to read about, and plans to finish the Colosseum model and possibly make a YouTube tour of it.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Thinks he can finish by Sunday but acknowledges a day off set him back
Wants to make one but suspects he'll delay it and move on to other projects first
Finds it genuinely fun and effective, like piecing together a puzzle

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log. It's 3.59 a.m. I'm actually very fresh. This is me in the morning, like pretty much just getting up. I've been up, I don't know, like a half hour or an hour or something like that so far. I can't remember exactly.

Um, uh, yeah, cause I got myself diurnalized. Um, normally if I do a video at four o'clock in the morning, it's cause I'm about to go to sleep. I've been working all the time, but yeah, now it's cause I'm getting up. Pretty cool. Um, so I did a video. I'm pretty high right now, but I'm not tired.

I did a video, um, a couple of days ago. Um, I can't remember. Yeah, I think I had segued into a bi-weekly history test page for a day and then I went back to, uh, the Coliseum and I'll get going on that bi-weekly history test, whatever, whenever I feel like it. Next time I Google Earth crashes or something, but I think I did a pretty good job of laying the groundwork.

Um, so, uh, yeah, I'm doing the stairs of the Coliseum and, uh, I got, you know, I finished all the flights of stairs to the top. I had to guess what the ones to the very top looked like because there wasn't a single illustration or description. Um, but you know that people went up there. Somebody mentioned that the roof of the Coliseum could hold 5,000 people.
So I just built some, um, I just built like a stair structure that goes like that. Like I think eight steps, eight steps. So, uh, to the roof and there were like six of them. And, um,

And then, uh, the stairs, there was a little bit of decision making I had to do about the stairs to the, uh, the top level. No, I mean the stairs to the, um, what would that be? The third level? Uh, the, the final stands before the, uh, the columns on the top.
Um, um, the stairs go like that, but I don't think, I think there were other stairs that went off the other way to take you to the, uh, the colonnade on the third level.
So, it's split off because you can see the holes on Google Earth, but then you see the diagrams going the other way.
So anyways, when I was done, uh, I just, right now I'm adding the other branch of stairs that take you into the colonnade.
And I just finished my first so that I don't get going on the other way. Then I'll be done, um, with all of the stairs.
And I can start working on the, uh, levels around the stairs. And it's, that's, and then after that, it's the stands.
And I guess I'm done. Um, but, um, I, uh, I think I predicted I could be done with it by Sunday,
But that was before I took a day off to do the bi-weekly history test page.
Um, but yeah, I don't think I'm that far away from finishing.
Um, but yeah, it's going to be interesting to see if I can actually take a tour inside of the Coliseum
And have it work and stuff. I think I can. Um, and, uh, and then maybe do a YouTube video for it about it.
But knowing me, I might not do that right away. I'd probably just finish the Coliseum and then
Not want to do a YouTube video about it because it'll take too long and then just try to finish
All the other Coliseums and the, uh, Circus Maximus. Um, yeah, not take as much time, but
I don't know, maybe I could make them with detail really quickly, you know, because Coliseum was
Particularly complicated. Um, yeah, and I, I went detailed with it. I don't have to go as detailed
With the other ones, but, um, and now, and then, uh, try to finish Rome. Um, yeah, if I can finish
